comment.body:
Hi Alma, Thx for taking time to help us learn. Current system the way I understand is where you share various reversion levels and some commentary behind those levels and but more or less we trade these reversion levels. 

Is there any value in monitor realtime changes some of the information you share? For examples looking at option depth heat map realtime because it changes as market progresses or is it static for the day? 

Second, if we should look at data like color, speed or others realtime then where can we monitor? I know you have your own scripts to calculate some of these. What do you recommend for us? 
Thx again for helping!

comment.body:
you got answer yesterday to this.
look, zero vanna lvl is a black-scholes based product, derived from the positioning, and has a significance as a result of the dealer flow.
there is multiple tools that meassure the IV, and there are multiple calculations, including simply the ATM vol, or the whole curve but weighted like in the VIX etc.
but these numbers have no effect on the hedging flow, if you think about it.
neither does realized vol. 
the only thing that has effect on the hedging dynamics is the vol smirk, and how it shifts at certain contracts.
to get an idea about that, you look at the fix strike vol. that will reveal the actual momentum of the volaitlity that matters here.
and so there you will gain some insigts about the vega of the contracts. 
as said, if you trade options, i assume you have some knowledge about it.

comment.body: Hi everyone. Wanted to share some open source code on trading view that I have found extremely helpful taking Alma's daily narrative and plotting it on the SPX chart. Attached is a screenshot from Friday. I've also included a link to the open source script. I use chat GPT to save the script as a base.  I then copy Alma's narrative and ask it to update the script each morning to only display the levels she provides that day. It works quite well. --> https://www.tradingview.com/script/mhCAFdh3-GEX-and-Vanna-Levels/

comment.body:
1) to see fixed strike vol goint down on a decline you have to see the put/call skew moving down, that means, customers short the skew --> selling hedges
2) however to see VIX down along with SPX down, you have to see the sell along the entire curve, including the far tail puts too
3) the small but 'dissonant' decline in COR3M says short correlation bets from the market side, bcs don't forget: this is IMPLIED correlation. So the market feels like long dispersion at the las hour...
4) RV only went up by 1.62% on an -1.71% decline. this is also the symptom of the predicted short skew phenomenon manifested in shorter downside speed I analysed on the morning post. 

I will scrutinize the next week more in depth, maybe on monday to wait for the freshest datas, but this just doesn't fit into the 'market fear', and 're-COVIDing' narrative for now.
